[142:43]Daryl Cooper was able to go on Tucker Carlson and Joe Rogan, two of the biggest podcasts in the world,

[142:49]and question the official narratives, the most important narratives about World War II,

[142:54]specifically the Holocaust and the role of Hitler and Winston Churchill.

[143:04]This is a summary of what he said.

[143:07]That's his quote. In September, 2024, Cooper appeared on the Tucker Carlson show where

[143:12]he made statements that drew widespread criticism from historians, Holocaust memorial organizations

[143:17]and political figures for promoting Holocaust revisionism. While discussing Nazi Germany's

[143:22]invasion of the Soviet Union, Cooper claimed that the Nazis went in with no plan for handling

[143:32]He further asserted that millions died because the Germans didn't have enough food to feed

[143:36]their own army, let alone prisoners, framing those atrocities as a result of logistical failure

[143:43]rather than a systematic genocide. And what amounts to Holocaust revisionism?

[143:49]This interpretation was widely condemned as a form of Holocaust revisionism for minimizing the

[143:55]ideological and systematic nature of Nazi crimes. And just for those that don't know,

[144:00]Holocaust denial has a specific definition.

[144:03]There's three things you can't question about the Holocaust.

[144:06]When they say you're a Holocaust denier,

[144:10]there are three things you are not allowed to deny

[144:13]about the Holocaust, and they are,

[144:16]one, that 6 million Jews died, at least 6 million.

[144:21]Two, that it was a systematic extermination

[144:26]meaning that the Nazis in a premeditated and willful way

[144:30]intended to murder all Jews,

[144:32]and three that they used gas chambers.

[144:37]They used poison gas chambers

[144:39]to murder en masse six million Jews.

[144:43]Those are the three things you cannot deny.

[144:46]And so one of the revisionist narratives

[144:50]questions that second point,

[144:52]which is that the Nazis did not intend

[144:54]It was not a willful plan or a premeditated plan to exterminate an entire race, but rather,

[145:22]they were running out of food,

[145:24]disease was spreading inside the camps.

[145:29]not because they were being gassed to death,

[145:31]but because they were starving,

[145:34]contracted, infectious diseases,

[145:38]and because there was a resource shortage.

[145:49]that narrative would say that it wasn't premeditated

[152:46]Cooper is the term limited hangout.

[152:51]I'll read to you the definition, this is also from Wikipedia, it says a limited hangout

[152:57]is a tactic used in media relations, perception management, politics and information management.

[153:05]According to Victor Marchetti, a former special assistant to the deputy director of the CIA,

[153:11]a limited hangout is this.

[153:13]He says it is spy jargon for a favorite and frequently used gimmick of clandestine professionals, secret professionals.

[153:22]He says when their veil of secrecy is shredded and they can no longer rely on a phony cover story to misinform the public,

[153:31]they resort to admitting, sometimes even volunteering, some of the truth while managing to withhold the key and damaging facts in the case.

[153:41]The public is so intrigued by the new information,

[153:45]they never think to pursue the matter further.

[153:50]And so what comes to mind when I hear that term
